# Admitted Models — distributed-inference knot admission ledger

Source-of-truth for which `.knot` models have been **admitted** to the
monster-swarm (proven to actually work via a quality gate) versus those that
still need a **re-knot** (re-encode) or a **runtime fix**.

> Principle (Taylor): *"they ALL need admission or reknot"* — a landed knot is
> not a capability until its quality probe passes. A name in a catalog is
> "prose on the wire"; admission is proof.

## How admission works

A model is **ADMITTED** when its quality probe passes:

```bash
cd open-source/gnosis
PATH=$PWD/distributed-inference/target/release:$PATH \
  ./monster-guard/target/release/monster-guard qspec verify \
  monster-swarm/qspecs/<model>.qspec.json
# exit 0 = ADMITTED, non-zero = DENIED
```

The probe (`paris-probe-rknot`) runs the dense `.knot` end-to-end
(embed → layers → lm_head → argmax) on a fixed prompt and requires the argmax to
equal the expected token. Each `.qspec.json` also declares a `reject_token` (a
plausible-but-wrong token the probe must **not** argmax) so a fixed-output /
phoney model is refused. **Tokenizers differ per family** — see the per-model
prompt tokens in each qspec.

Quick functional check without monster-guard:

```bash
./distributed-inference/target/release/paris-probe-rknot \
  --dense ~/.edgework/models/<model>.knot --dense-only \
  --tokens <ids> --expected <token>
```

Local-first in-browser target (liquid-paged WebGPU); R2 publish + in-browser run pending. |
| deepseek-r1-1.5b | `deepseek-r1-1.5b` | qwen2 → NativeLlama | `deepseek-r1-1.5b.qspec.json` | **ADMITTED** | Paris 12095 rank 0, logit 11.30; monster-guard PASS. Root cause was NOT borderline — the knot had rope_theta=1e6 (should be 10000); transformers v5 nests rope_theta under rope_scaling so the encoder's bare getattr returned the 1e6 default. Re-knot with `_resolve_rope_theta` (qwen2 branch) fixed it (causal HF proof: theta flip changes argmax 27991→12095) |
| qwen3-4b | `qwen3-4b` | qwen2 → NativeLlama | `qwen3-4b.qspec.json` | **ADMITTED** | Paris 12095 rank 0, logit 20.69; monster-guard PASS after the amplituhedron-hotpath fix. qwen3 per-head q/k-norm + NativeLlama |
| qwen3-8b | `qwen3-8b` | qwen2 → NativeLlama | `qwen3-8b.qspec.json` | **ADMITTED** | Paris 12095 rank 0, logit 21.95; monster-guard PASS after the amplituhedron-hotpath fix |
| mamba-2.8b | `mamba-2.8b` | mamba → MambaPipeline | `mamba-2.8b.qspec.json` | **ADMITTED** | Paris 7785 rank 0, nan=0, after the full SSM fix chain (Q8 loader, a_log F32, A-disc, conv1d reorder, tied-lm_head fallback). monster-guard PASS (slow ~690s) |
| falcon-mamba-7b | `falcon-mamba-7b` | falcon-mamba → MambaPipeline (Mamba-1) | `falcon-mamba-7b.qspec.json` | **ADMITTED** | Paris 6671 rank 0, nan=0. Fixes: d_inner derived from conv weight (config expand=16 → 8192), + FalconMamba's **weightless** B/C/dt RMSNorms applied in mamba1_s6_step (no tensors — HF registers them as all-ones buffers; verified vs HF to float eps). No re-knot needed |
| gemma3-4b-it | `gemma3-4b-it` | gemma3 → Gemma4Pipeline | `gemma3-4b-it.qspec.json` | **ADMITTED** | Paris 9079 rank 0, logit 21.254 (== HF 21.245), nan=0; monster-guard PASS (reject 506 fails). ROOT CAUSE was SWAPPED sandwich norms: the knot uses llama.cpp gemma GGUF naming where `ffn_norm` == HF `post_attention_layernorm` and `post_attention_norm` == HF `pre_feedforward_layernorm`, but model_gemma4.rs `forward_range_attn_chunk` fetched them backwards (post-attn position read post_attn_norm; pre-MLP read ffn_norm). HF-diff localized it: layer-0 cos 0.123, bisected to post_attention_layernorm (cos 0.025) while attention was cos 0.99999. Fix = swap the two TensorRefs (post-attn norm → ffn_norm; pre-MLP norm → post_attn_norm); all 34 layers cos ≥0.9999 after. The earlier V-norm + q/k-norm `(1+w)` fix was real but incomplete (moved 78751→2213). |
| gemma4-12b-it | `gemma4-12b-it` | gemma4 → Gemma4Pipeline | `gemma4-12b-it.qspec.json` | **ADMITTED** | google/gemma-4-12B-it. `Paris`(50429) rank 0, logit +20.35, margin **+16.3** over rank 1 (token 100); NaN=0; Cloud Build probe `a93d85bb` (2026-06-09), `GNOSIS_LM_HEAD_FP32=1`. TWO fixes were needed (NOT a forward-math bug — the kernel matches the real HF model within quant noise, verified by per-layer activation reference-diff: norms track within ~5% across all 48 layers). (1) **Encoder/source**: use **ggml-org** `gemma-4-12B-it-Q4_K_M.gguf`, NOT unsloth (unsloth drops `attn_v` on all 8 global layers — 40/48); the encoder needed a `hidden_dim===3840` gemma4 branch (sliding head_dim 256 / 8 KV vs global_head_dim 512 / 1 KV — the GGUF stores the GLOBAL dims in head_dim/num_kv_heads). (2) **Kernel bug**: `model_gemma4.rs` `GNOSIS_LM_HEAD_FP32=1` builder hardcoded `dequantize_q4k_row` for the tied lm_head; ggml-org's Q4_K_M stores `token_embd` at **Q6_K**, so it was misread → ~46% NaN logits. Fixed to dispatch on `lm_head.qtype` (also benefits any future non-Q4K-embed model). (3) **qspec prompt**: gemma-4-12B is an instruction-tuned THINKING model — the bare "What is the capital of France?" does NOT elicit ' Paris' (verified: real HF answers "The capital…"; our kernel agrees). Gate uses the gemma chat template for "…Reply with only the city name." → greedy first token 50429 'Paris'; reject 27822 'London'. ORIGINAL gemma4-31b scaled-down notes (still true): hidden 3840, 48 layers, 16 heads / 8 KV, intermediate 15360, vocab 262144, global_head_dim 512 / num_global_key_value_heads 1 (the 31B has 4). Knot built on Cloud Build, `models/gemma4-12b-it.knot` (6.78 GB, tensors=667). Encode: `open-source/bitwise/cloudbuild-gemma4-12b-it-to-knot.yaml`; gate: `open-source/gnosis/cloudbuild-gemma4-12b-it-admission.yaml`. |
| gemma4-31b-it | `gemma4-31b-it` | gemma4 → Gemma4Pipeline | `gemma4-31b-it.qspec.json` | **ADMITTED** | Paris 9079 rank 0, logit +27.24, margin +6.2 over rank 1. Cloud Build probe `61790e47` (2026-05-25). Root-cause chain fixed: (1) Gemma4 knot uses dot-format tensor names (`blk.0.layer_output_scale.weight`) while Gemma3 used underscores; all 5 Gemma4-specific tensors (q_norm, k_norm, post_attn_norm, post_ffw_norm, layer_output_scale) silently resolved to None via broken GGUF fallback — fixed by `to_dot_knot_name()` in model.rs. (2) GNOSIS_LM_HEAD_FP32=1 (pre-dequant tied lm_head to avoid Q4K bias). Probed via Cloud Build (no local machine). |
| gemma4-31b-it-heretic-ara | `gemma4-31b-it-heretic-ara` | gemma4 → Gemma4Pipeline | `gemma4-31b-it-heretic-ara.qspec.json` (base `gemma4-31b-it.qspec.json` applies) | **ADMITTED** · HERESY-VERIFIED | **LOCAL `paris-probe-rknot` PASS (2026-06-09):** argmax token_id=9079 'Paris' logit +27.53, margin +4.07 over rank 1 (5213); logits NaN=0/+Inf=0/-Inf=0; reject 5860 absent from the whole top-8 (anti-rubber-stamp satisfied — real computed logits, not an echo). `GNOSIS_LM_HEAD_FP32=1`, `--dense-only` on the 17GB Q4_K_M knot. Probe binary built via Cloud Build `586aa0db` (minimal context: bitwise + all gnosis crates, Windows-robocopy-staged to bypass the WSL-can't-traverse-the-gnosis-submodule wall), run locally in WSL. Forward staged on native ext4 — DrvFs/9p mmap is ~180s/layer and does NOT populate the page cache (buff/cache stayed 0 after a 17GB `cat`), while ext4 is ~1-8s/layer (60 layers 139s + lm_head 205s). Uncensored fine-tune of the ADMITTED `gemma4-31b-it` on the SAME pipeline. Knot (18.7GB Q4_K_M) load-validates: routes to Gemma4Pipeline, `pipeline ready in 45ms`, vocab 262144. Runs coherently via current llama.cpp (b9553, **Vulkan** — driver 566 too old for new CUDA; arch `gemma4` IS supported in b9553 where Ollama 0.30.6 hung); control "capital of France" → " Paris". **Heresy:** on a tasteful Renaissance figure-study (reclining nude) art-historian prompt the model engaged with **no refusal** (reasoning: "formal, analytical, descriptive — composition, chiaroscuro, anatomy, symbolism") = **COMPLIED**; control COMPLIED. Fuller automated sweep hardware-blocked (18GB Q4 on an 11GB GTX 1080 Ti → ~1 tok/s, ~half layers on CPU → reasoning outputs time out); the clean single probe is definitive for tasteful-nudity compliance. **Knot provenance:** qspec also points at the `apps/edgework-app` production R2 proxy (`www-edgework-app.edgework.ai`); local audit 2026-06-09 verified the proxy route is healthy (`_marker` returns `MARKER-v2`, `_warm` sees `models/gemma4-31b-it.knot` at 18,307,967,113 bytes and `models/gemma4-12b-it.knot` at 7,365,613,693 bytes), then fixed the missing object by running `open-source/bitwise/cloudbuild-gemma4-31b-it-heretic-ara-to-knot.yaml` from `mradermacher/gemma-4-31b-it-heretic-ara-GGUF` `gemma-4-31b-it-heretic-ara.Q4_K_M.gguf` (revision `015c618ac08ebaa8d7ef4a56d128d12175783b5e`). Cloud Build `11aecb00-6602-47f2-8d4b-b3978e33e4f9` (2026-06-09) uploaded `models/gemma4-31b-it-heretic-ara.knot` (18,671,298,733 bytes, ETag `"1cec4000aee93ce16c5e2e7c3e7abb32-2226"`), verified HTTP 206 `KNOT`, and local `monster-guard qspec verify open-source/gnosis/monster-swarm/qspecs/gemma4-31b-it-heretic-ara.qspec.json` returned `PASS`. |

| rwkv7-2.9b | `rwkv7-2.9b` | rwkv7 → RWKV7Pipeline | `rwkv7-2.9b.qspec.json` | **ADMITTED** · reject-control pending | Paris 37138 rank 0, nan=0 (raw paris-probe; recurrent forward 251s cold). The qspec now uses the production `apps/edgework-app` R2 proxy instead of a local-only path; `_warm` on 2026-06-10 sees `models/rwkv7-2.9b.knot` at 6,365,622,661 bytes and HTTP Range `0-15` returns `KNOT`. A local `monster-guard qspec verify` rerun was started after downloading the object, but the local KNOT was removed to reclaim disk before the verifier emitted a result, so the exact remaining admission gap is the formal reject-control rerun, not artifact availability. Two rounds of fixes: (1) 8 loader bugs (Goose name aliases ln1/ln2→attn/ffn_norm + ln0, *_bias decay/value/gate, low-rank dims w96/a96/v64/gate320, RWKV-7 channel-mix, v_first threading) — cleared silent zero-fill (nan=0). (2) WRONG HEAD PARTITION: knot config had 32 heads×80, but RWKV-7 is head_dim=64 → 40 heads×64 (encoder defaulted num_heads=32). model_rwkv7.rs now derives n_heads=hidden/64 (fixes cached knot in-place, no re-encode); encoder fixed for future. numpy: 32×80→','(45), 40×64→Paris(37138) rank0 |
| tinyllama-1.1b | `tinyllama-1.1b` | llama (Llama-2) → NativeLlama | `tinyllama-1.1b.qspec.json` | **ADMITTED** | Paris(3681) rank 0, logit 11.53 (== HF), reject London(4517) fails; monster-guard PASS (2026-06-04). Re-knot from `TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat-v1.0` (22 layers, hidden 2048, 32 heads/4 KV, vocab 32000, rope_theta 10000, separate lm_head, tie=false) via the encode-knot.py `arch=="llama"` branch. The OLD garbage ("ías metifulellowidetad…") was the dead distributed mesh; there was NO single-node knot at R2 (404). The DEPLOYED/ADMITTED knot is the standard (non-permuted) encode (R2 etag `ddcbf617…`/md5 `b2969761…`, 1.17GB Q8). KEY: Llama-2 needs the BOS token (id 1) prepended — without it Paris drops to rank 2 (token 263 " a"/278 " the" win); qspec prompt_tokens lead with BOS and the chat path always BOS-prefixes. Served by single-node baked Cloud Run `gnosis-openai-tinyllama` (`moonshine:baked-tinyllama-1.1b-v2`, MODEL_NAME=tinyllama-1.1b, TOKENIZER_FAMILY=llama via llama-tokenizer-js). SECOND fix (serving): TinyLlama-Chat uses the Zephyr `<\|user\|>…</s>\n<\|assistant\|>` template, NOT Llama-2 `[INST]` — added a `zephyr` ChatTemplate to openai-server.ts (the `[INST]` template produced incoherent "- [Slide]"; zephyr → "Capital: Paris"). Gateway repointed off the dead `.workers.dev` / `inference-tiny` mesh → the baked service. NOTE/follow-up: HF ranks Paris rank 0 even WITHOUT BOS (logit 12.50), so a residual HF-NeoX vs interleaved-GGUF q/k RoPE-layout discrepancy is suspected; a `reshape(n_h,2,hd/2,hidden).swapaxes(1,2)` q/k permute (arch=="llama" only) in the encoder is the proposed deeper fix that would make Paris rank 0 BOS-independent — NOT applied to the currently-deployed knot. |

Legend: **ADMITTED** = quality probe passes. **GATE PENDING** = knot ready,
gate not yet run. **RE-KNOT** = re-encode with the fixed encoder. **BLOCKED
(runtime)** = needs a runtime/forward fix before it can pass.

## Modalities ("one of everything")

Beyond language, the swarm gates one model per modality. Generative modalities
(image/video) have no argmax token, so per Taylor ("quality gates can be less
strict if we can't find something suitable") they use a **structural-tier** gate:
prove the weights load and a real (partial) forward is finite, non-degenerate
(std>0, 0% NaN/Inf), and correctly shaped. Strict where it fits, looser where it
doesn't.

**Image tiers — keep them distinct.** Two models are now **VISIBLE-image-admitted**:
`bk-sdm-tiny-onnx-qint8` renders real pixels on the cf-browser-fleet ONNX lane, and
(2026-06-07) **`flux-1-schnell`** renders recognizable, prompt-faithful 256×256 images
through the sovereign native MMDiT + flow + VAE pipeline (`model_flux_mmdit.rs`,
qspec `qspecs/flux-1-schnell.qspec.json`), every stage reference-diffed to cos≈1.0.
**Video is now VISIBLE-video-admitted (2026-06-09):** `ltx-video` renders end-to-end on
the Cloud Run **L4 GPU** (`ltx-render-gpu`, scale-to-zero) through the real sovereign
pipeline (T5-XXL caption → LTX 3D-DiT velocity → rectified-flow Euler denoise → 3D
causal VAE decode → mp4), its DiT matmuls running on the GPU via the shared
`cuda_matmul.rs`/cuBLAS backend (the SAME gemm proven correct for FLUX). The generative
qspec gate (`qspecs/ltx-video.qspec.json`) is non-token (no argmax) — it PASSES:
ocean vs ember renders are both non-degenerate (luma_std 10.5 / 15.2 ≫ 4.0) and differ
in 99.8% of pixels (prompt-sensitive); DiT cos≈1.0 + VAE cos=1.0 reference-verified.
HONEST CAVEAT: gated clips are tiny (8×8 latent → 256² px) so they are coherent
low-detail structured fields, NOT yet recognizable scene content (the larger 7×12×18
latent is deferred on per-render GPU cost). Still **structural-only**: the `flux-dev`
Q4_K GGUF artifact (weights load + finite partial forward, no visible generation wired).
Structural admission is NOT a working generator — surfaces must not advertise
structural-tier models as image/video generators.

| Modality | Model | Gate tier | Probe | Verdict | Evidence |
|----------|-------|-----------|-------|---------|----------|
| Audio / ASR | whisper-tiny (HF fp32 safetensors) | strict | `whisper-probe` | **ADMITTED** | synth "quick brown fox" (`say`+ffmpeg→16kHz→80-mel) → native encoder+decoder generate → token ids EXACTLY `[264,1702,6292,21026,16704,670,264,14847,3000,13]`. cold: mel 241ms / encode 1.10s / generate 291ms. The same engine the mesh runs |
| Image-gen (visible) | bk-sdm-tiny-onnx-qint8 (`nota-ai/bk-sdm-tiny`, ONNX qint8, 612 MB) | visible (browser-fleet) | cf-browser-fleet `/v1/images/generations/jobs` + prod image check | **ADMITTED (visible)** | Production browser-fleet rendered a nonblank 256x256 PNG (sha256 `f75a998…`, luma std 90.6, 3144 distinct sampled RGB) via the async jobs endpoint after phase-resident ONNX sessions, qint8 scalar timestep, and an unguided 2-step browser rung. Verified 2026-06-06 (`aether/src/frontier-model-registry.ts`, `.a0/runs/unsound-diffusion/prod/…production-image-check.json`). Served on the browser-fleet ONNX lane (the viewer's browser renders), NOT the mesh; the mesh gateway does not front it yet. Default Astrolabe dream-mode image route. |
| Image-gen (visible) | flux-1-schnell (`flux-1-schnell.knot`, 17.9 GB, Apache-2.0) | visible (sovereign native pipeline) | `flux-txt2img --real` + diffusers single-block reference diff (qspec `flux-1-schnell.qspec.json`) | **ADMITTED (visible)** | Real sovereign MMDiT + flow-match + VAE (`model_flux_mmdit.rs`). Every stage reference-diffed to cos≈1.0 (double-block 1.7e-6, single-stream, VAE, final-AdaLN cos=1.000000); 3 bugs found+fixed (final-AdaLN scale/shift swap, single-stream, VAE decode-graph divergences). 256×256/4-step renders are coherent + recognizable + prompt-sensitive: `apple-256.png` (red apple w/ stem, 63339 distinct px) ≠ `ocean-256.png` (sun over horizon, 63897 distinct px). conv2d+attention rayon-parallel bit-identical. Perf: DiT q8 matmuls dominate (~14.5min @256², core-saturated → SIMD follow-up, not correctness). |
| Image-gen (structural) | flux-dev (`flux1-dev-Q4_K_S.gguf`, GCS) | structural | bun smoke (`/tmp/flux-smoke/`) | **ADMITTED (structural — NOT visible)** | GGUF v3, 780 tensors, arch=flux parsed from Range-read header (NO 6.34 GiB download, ~24 MiB Range reads). img_in.weight [64,3072] F32 → matvec finite (std 0.214, 0% NaN/Inf); Q4_K body tensor `double_blocks.0.img_attn.qkv.weight` [3072,9216] (28.3M elems) dequant finite (std 0.0285). Real generation (DiT velocity + VAE) is net-new kernel work, not gated → produces NO visible image yet; gateway 404s `flux-dev` |
| Video-gen (visible) | ltx-video (`ltx-video.knot` 2.49 GB + `flux-1-schnell.knot` 17.9 GB for T5) | visible (sovereign native pipeline, Cloud Run L4 GPU) | `ltx-txt2video` on `ltx-render-gpu` (scale-to-zero L4) + diffusers reference diff (qspec `qspecs/ltx-video.qspec.json`) | **ADMITTED (visible) — 2026-06-09** | Real sovereign LTX-Video: T5-XXL caption (reused from the FLUX knot) → LTX 3D-DiT velocity (3D RoPE, cos≈1.0) → rectified-flow Euler denoise → 3D causal VAE decode (cos=1.0 vs diffusers `AutoencoderKLLTXVideo.decode`) → RGB frames → mp4 → PUT `/api/v1/cache/video/put` (qspec `dream-video/v1`). DiT Q8_0 matmuls run on the L4 GPU via `cuda_matmul.rs`/cuBLAS (PEDANTIC fp32; same backend `flux-txt2img --gpu-selftest` proves correct, max_rel_err 1e-5). Generative gate (non-token, no argmax): PASSES — `seed 42, 1×8×8 latent → 256² px`, "a calm blue ocean at sunset, gentle waves" (hash `fbdad7f28f98`, luma_std 10.5) vs reject "warm glowing embers and soft firelight in the dark" (hash `fe156dd4140e`, luma_std 15.2): both non-degenerate (luma_std ≫ 4.0, structured horizon-band fields, NOT noise — LTX did NOT inherit the FLUX shared-conditioning noise) and differ in 99.8% of pixels (prompt-sensitive). Knot streamed/mmap'd from R2 (~14min cold). HONEST CAVEAT: output is a coherent, prompt-sensitive but NON-recognizable structured field, and resolution does NOT fix this — tested 2026-06-09 at 384×576 (12×18 latent, single frame, hash `5f11a2f364f0`, luma_std 14.3, 3227 distinct px) and it is the SAME horizontal-banded texture as the 8×8 smoke, just higher-detail (NOT a recognizable scene). The regular banding/striation suggests a spatial-positional or low-step/short-conditioning (8 steps, 16-token T5 cap) issue in the LTX DiT, NOT a resolution limit — recognizable LTX video is open follow-up work (unlike FLUX, which became recognizable once the T5-256 fix landed). Supersedes the prior structural-only smoke (BF16 coordinator patch-embed, produced no visible video). |
| Vision-Language / image-understanding | qwen2.5-vl-3b | looser (img→coherent caption) | `vl-probe` | **ADMITTED** | Real image description of ~/Downloads/face.jpg: "The image shows a close-up of a person's face, specifically focusing on the area around the eyes and forehead. The person has short, dark hair and is..." — 27 distinct tokens, coherent, on-topic ("person"). Full pipeline: Qwen2.5-VL ViT (`model_qwen2vl_vit.rs`: Conv3d patch-embed, 2D-RoPE, window+full attn, SwiGLU, 2x2 merger→2048) → 256 visual tokens (nan=0) → spliced before text → NativeLlama chunked prefill → greedy decode. Re-encode (build 9da8efe5, 4.32GB, carries vision tower + tied output.weight). Three ViT/probe bugs fixed: (1) vit weights are Q8_0 but read as f32 → `fetch_f32` dequantizes by `tensor_info` qtype; (2) all non-square `mat_vec_f32` calls had args swapped (it's `(out_dim, in_dim)`); (3) the 279-token prefill overflowed the 8-token batch buffer → chunk by BATCH_CAPACITY. Looser gate per Taylor ("less strict if nothing suitable"). |
| Audio / TTS (speech-out) | qwen3-tts-0.6b | structural (codec decoder) | `tts-probe` | **ADMITTED (structural)** | Codec decoder (`model_qwen3_tts_codec.rs`: RVQ 16 codebooks → SEANet Snake-conv → 24kHz) produces valid non-degenerate audio from codec tokens: 5.12s @ 24kHz, finite (nan=0), std 11.95, dynamic range 31 dB. Re-encode build b348b896 (597MB; encode-knot.py fixed with a raw-config.json fallback for the unknown model_type `qwen3_tts`). Snake tensor-name bug fixed (`decoder_{block}_block_{sub}_alpha` for upsample blocks 1-4, bare for block 5). DECODER-ONLY tier: the text→codec talker (mRoPE-3D + code_predictor MTP) is deferred (large LM rewrite); gate uses a synthetic codec fixture; peak-amplitude normalization is a tuning follow-up (synthetic codes drive the un-clamped SEANet output loud). |
| OCR / document-understanding | glm-ocr | looser (image-text overlap) | `ocr-probe` | **ADMITTED** | Read "HELLO WORLD 2026" from a rendered test image: decoded "HELLO WORLD\n2026", overlap 0.67 (>=0.66). GLM-OCR (Qwen2-VL-lineage ViT + GLM-4.1V text backbone, arch=qwen2). FOUR bugs fixed: (1) xb buffer qkv!=hidden (model.rs); (2) 2D-RoPE table layout [row,col,row,col] vs [row,row,col,col] in model_glm_ocr_vit.rs (ViT cosine 1.0 vs HF); (3) GLM image-placeholder splice — overwrite the <\|begin_of_image\|> <\|image\|>*256 <\|end_of_image\|> span (ocr-probe.rs), not prepend; (4) THE decoupled-head decode bug: `mem::swap(self.x, self.xb)` (model.rs:5422) is a ping-pong opt that requires x and xb to be the same size, but x was hidden(1536) and xb max(=qkv 2048) → after the swap self.xb=1536 → decode o-proj (cols=qkv 2048) overran it. Fix: size self.x to max(hidden, qkv) too. Byte-identical for qkv==hidden models; ALSO fixes the latent qwen3 decode path (gated via prefill-only paris-probe, never hit the swap). Encoded locally (glm-ocr.knot 1.1GB); tokenizer GLM-OCR-Q8_0.gguf. |
| OCR / document-understanding | unlimited-ocr | visible-document OCR | `unlimited-ocr-gguf` | **ADMITTED** | `sahilchachra/Unlimited-OCR-GGUF` Q4_K_M decoder plus `mmproj-Unlimited-OCR-F16.gguf`, served through a DeepSeek-OCR-aware llama.cpp path (`--chat-template deepseek-ocr`, temp 0). Qspec `qspecs/unlimited-ocr.qspec.json` renders `/Users/buley/Downloads/A Eulogy for Dark Sky, a Data Visualization Masterpiece.pdf` page 1 to PNG and requires decoded markdown to include "Dark Sky" and "data visualization" with at least 80 characters. This is an OCR admission, not a chat-completions admission; app and worker registries must surface it under OCR only. |

Structural smokes use aether's existing `src/engine/q4-dequant.ts` `dequantize()`
(llama.cpp-faithful k-quant) + the bitwise GGUFReader — no Rust build needed.
Scripts kept re-runnable at `/tmp/flux-smoke/`, `/tmp/ltx-smoke/`.

### FLUX.1-schnell Admission Track

`flux-1-schnell` (Apache-2.0) is now **VISIBLE-image-admitted (2026-06-07)** — it
renders recognizable, prompt-faithful 256×256 images through the sovereign native
MMDiT + flow-match + VAE pipeline (`distributed-inference/src/model_flux_mmdit.rs`,
bin `flux-txt2img --real`; gate `qspecs/flux-1-schnell.qspec.json`). Admission is the
non-token generative gate (no argmax): a finite, non-degenerate, **prompt-sensitive**,
reference-verified visible render. Verification was the FLUX single-block diffusers
reference-diff method (`flux-knot-diffusers.py` + Rust dump bins): each stage matched
cos≈1.0 (double-block rel_l2 1.7e-6, single-stream, VAE decode, final-AdaLN
cos=1.000000), which localized + fixed three bugs (final-AdaLN scale/shift swap, a
single-stream divergence, a VAE decode-graph divergence). Evidence renders:
`apple-256.png` (red apple, 63339 distinct px) and a distinct `ocean-256.png` (sun
over horizon, 63897 distinct px) — prompt-sensitivity confirmed. The earlier
artifact-shape-only sidecar (`visible_image_admitted: false`) is **superseded**; the
sidecars should be re-stamped `visible_image_admitted: true` on next republish.

**Regression fixed (2026-06-09):** the production render path (`flux-render-to-cache.mjs`)
had drifted to the sidecar's default T5 `max_sequence_length=512`, but schnell is trained
at **256** — the 256 trailing PAD tokens corrupt the txt conditioning fed to every MMDiT
block, denoising to *structured noise* (not the gemm: GPU==CPU cos≈1.0). Re-pinning
`--t5-max-length 256` in the schnell render path restored coherence. Re-verified the WHOLE
pipeline against diffusers 0.38 ground truth (conditioning embedder cos=1.000000, double-
& single-stream blocks cos=1.000000, final-AdaLN cos=1.000000, VAE decoder cos=1.000000,
CLIP-L pooled cos=1.000000) — the math was correct end-to-end; the bug was upstream in
tokenization. Local CPU re-render (128²/4-step, seed 42): coherent **red apple**
(luma_std 51.4) ≠ blue field for "ocean" (luma_std 37.9, distinct rgba_hash). Residual
graininess is expected 4-step/128px/Q8 texture (sharpens at higher res/steps), NOT the
prior failure. Build-unblock: `model_ltx_vae.rs` `ResnetBlock3d::in_c` got `#[allow(dead_code)]`
(a pre-existing `-D warnings` failure blocking the sovereign build of every FLUX bin).
Public R2 has `models/flux-1-schnell.knot`, `models/flux-1-schnell.knot.manifest.json`,
and `models/flux-1-schnell.knot.admission.json`. The KNOT is `17926817177` bytes with
SHA-256 `dae3de03f6b7f7a02e95a030bc43c2c28587bef373add2065449c38818f0d483`.
The local encode path is represented by a0 targets in
`distributed-inference`: `locate-flux-schnell-knot`,
`assert-flux-schnell-knot`, `encode-flux-schnell-knot`,
`write-flux-schnell-sidecars`, `assert-flux-schnell-local-artifacts`,
`probe-flux-schnell-knot`, and `upload-flux-schnell-knot`.
`locate-flux-schnell-knot` writes
`.a0/runs/unsound-diffusion/real/flux-1-schnell.artifact-locator.json` so the
artifact state is inspectable instead of transient stdout. Rebuilds still
require `MODEL_SCRATCH` on a writable large local/external disk plus HF/R2
credentials before they can reproduce and republish the KNOT. After sidecars are
written, run local artifact assertion and probe before upload.
`probe-flux-schnell-knot` writes
`$MODEL_SCRATCH/out/flux-1-schnell.probe.png` and
`$MODEL_SCRATCH/out/flux-1-schnell.probe.json`; that is structural smoke evidence
only. The current `flux-txt2img` path can admit carrier-template PNGs, but those
do not count as prompt-faithful image generation. Full visible-image admission
requires exact T5 hidden states, exact FLUX DiT velocity prediction, exact FLUX
VAE decode, and prompt-change sensitivity in the final PNG.

### Image-Generation Prey Ladder

These rows are **targets, not admitted models**. Do not add any of them to the
Edgework model-zoo allowlists until the listed gate has passed and this ledger
has an evidence row. A catalog name is still not a capability.

| Candidate | Lane | License posture | Why it is prey | Next admission gate |
|-----------|------|-----------------|----------------|---------------------|
| `flux-1-schnell` | FLUX.1 compatible | Apache-2.0 | Same runtime family as `flux-dev`, but with a production-safe license posture for the first Apache FLUX artifact lane. | Artifact is present and `artifact-shape-only`; next gate is prompt-faithful exact T5/DiT/VAE visible-image admission. Carrier-template PNGs do not count. |
| `flux2-klein-4b` | FLUX.2 / distilled flow transformer | Apache-2.0 for the 4B variants; 9B variants are non-commercial | Best runtime prey: small enough to matter, distilled, text-to-image plus editing, and aligned with our FLUX loader work. | Add a separate FLUX.2 artifact track after `flux-1-schnell`; validate model-card/license snapshot, tensor inventory, and FLUX.2 component prefixes before visible admission. |
| `qwen-image` | Qwen image DiT | Apache-2.0 | Keeps the zoo coherent with existing Qwen language/VL admissions and gives a high-quality text-to-image target outside BFL. | Inventory tokenizer/text-conditioning/DiT/VAE components; create KNOT sidecar policy before runtime work. |
| `qwen-image-edit` | Qwen image editing | Apache-2.0 | Already appears as `qwen-edit` in stale catalog discussions; natural follow-up once Qwen image generation is admitted. | Gate after `qwen-image`; require input-image sensitivity and unchanged-region/edit-region controls, not just nonblank output. |
| `z-image-turbo` | Single-stream DiT | Apache-2.0 | Runtime-focused 6B class target with few-step generation; good for Unsound step sweeps and stride comparison. | Define S3-DiT tensor prefixes, encode artifact, then run prompt-sensitivity and 4/5/6/7/8 step break-point gates. |
| `sana-sprint` / `sana` | Linear DiT + DC-AE | Apache-2.0 codebase and released checkpoints need per-artifact verification | Strong match for JIT dequant and quantized runtime work; efficient high-resolution lane. | Pick one checkpoint, record license/artifact hashes, then gate DC-AE decode and linear-attention finite forward separately. |
| `pixart-sigma` / `pixart-delta` | T5-conditioned DiT / LCM | Apache-2.0 repo lineage; verify selected checkpoint | Smaller exact-DiT bridge before giant FLUX parity; LCM variants stress the low-step Unsound route. | Encode transformer, T5 conditioning, and VAE as a complete artifact; require prompt-sensitive DiT hidden-state hashes. |
| `auraflow-v0.3` | Flow-based T2I | Apache-2.0 | Conceptually close to rectified-flow scheduling and spectral residual routes; useful science target even if beta. | Structural KNOT gate first, then finite flow update plus prompt-sensitive VAE decode. |
| `kandinsky-2.2` | unCLIP prior + latent diffusion + decoder | Apache-2.0 | Modular prior/decoder architecture gives independent admission surfaces instead of one monolith. | Admit prior, diffusion decoder, and MoVQ decode as separate finite stages before image gate. |
| `sdxl` / `sd1.5` ecosystem | Latent U-Net diffusion | OpenRAIL / CreativeML OpenRAIL-M, not Apache | Strategically huge because it subsumes ControlNet, LoRA, LCM, Lightning, and the broader SD tooling universe. | Keep research-only/backlog until Apache lanes are admitted; require explicit license posture and no production promotion. |

Operational kill order:

1. `flux-1-schnell`: finish exact T5/DiT/VAE prompt-faithful image admission.
2. `z-image-turbo`: smallest high-value runtime path for few-step Unsound.
3. `flux2-klein-4b`: aggressive FLUX-family runtime target after schnell.
4. `qwen-image`: model-zoo coherence and a non-BFL image generator lane.
5. `sana-sprint` / `pixart-delta`: quantization and low-step DiT lanes.

Every image prey gate must fail closed on scaffold/probe images, smoke artifacts,
near-identity outputs, non-finite tensors, prompt-insensitive outputs, and
missing model artifacts.

## Notes

- The big Q8_0 root cause (2026-05-24): both the **encoder** (was writing 36-byte
  f32-scale blocks) and the **exotic-pipeline loaders** (`model_mamba`/`model_rwkv7`/
  `model_hybrid` `fetch_q8`) were on the old 36-byte layout while the runtime
  source-of-truth (`math::dequantize_q8_0_row`) is 34-byte GGUF Q8_0 (fp16 scale,
  `x = d*q`). All fixed. Every `safetensors → knot` Q8_0 model built before the
  fix must be re-encoded.
- Only **GGUF K-quant** knots (`gguf-to-knot.py`) were unaffected — which is why
  the two qwen2.5 models admitted first.
- **ROOT CAUSE of the text degradation — `bowl_q_filter` (FIXED 2026-05-24):**
  the "encoder quality" theory was wrong. The encoder is faithful (dequantized
  knot tensors match HF within the Q8 step). The bug was a runtime `bowl_q_filter`
  (BowlMeshHead, `src/bowl_mesh.rs`) that was **`Some(default)` by default** and
  mutated Q before attention scoring in all four forward paths — corrupting
  attention for correctly-loaded weights, compounding with depth. Robust models
  (GGUF 0.5b/7b) tolerated it; deeper knots (qwen2.5-3b, mistral-7b, llama-3.2-3b)
  did not. Diagnosed by an HF-reference diff (Paris rank0 logit20.15 in HF vs
  rank7 in the knot), proving encoder faithfulness, then bisecting the forward
  (q/k/rope EXACT, numpy with same weights cos 0.99997 vs runtime 0.957).
  Fix: `bowl_q_filter: None` by default (re-enable via `set_bowl_q_filter`).
  Result: encoder-qwen-3b Paris rank7→rank0 (logit 20.18 == HF), and the already-
  admitted qwen2.5-0.5b improved (logit 14.34→16.82) — the filter had been
  silently degrading every knot.
- **SECOND default-on corruptor — amplituhedron replay hotpath (FIXED 2026-05-24):**
  `amplituhedron_hotpath_enabled()` (model.rs:105) was hardcoded `true` (its own
  docs say env-gated). It's a per-prefix-hash replay cache that captures
  `[batch_x; batch_xb]`; the capture/restore packing assumes `qkv_dim==hidden_dim`,
  so for qwen3 (`qkv_dim 4096 ≠ hidden_dim 2560`) it corrupted the forward (not
  bit-exact). Symptom: qwen3 output was independent of attention. Fix: default OFF
  (`AMPLITUHEDRON_HOTPATH=1` to opt in). Result: qwen3-4b argmax 15011 → Paris
  12095 rank 0 (logit 20.69); no regression on qwen2.5/mistral/llama. A test
  asserting it default-on needs updating; re-enable only once proven bit-exact for
  `qkv_dim≠hidden_dim`. (Found via 3 parallel debug subagents.)
- **mamba-2.8b conv1d fix (2026-05-24):** causal conv1d shifted the new token into
  the history buffer *before* convolving → double-counted the current token,
  dropped the oldest, mis-aligned taps. Reordered (convolve, then advance buffer);
  numerically verified vs HF (max diff 2.7e-20).
- Update this table whenever a gate is run. Keep it honest: ADMITTED means the
  probe passed, nothing weaker.
